explanation_box.text: It looks like a juvenile which would make it too young to produce many offspring.
It is at it's peak fertility mid summer as can be estimated by the leaves around it.
This one is unhealthy as can be seen in the spots on it's skin. This prevents it from being as fertile as a healthy specimin.
This one is bright red which may be a sign of older age which would bring down it's fertility score.
Because she only has 3 legs, she is unable to get away from all the males chasing her, and thus get's pregnant more often.
She has discovered that she likes sex a lot as can be interpreted by her big smile. With no contraception available, she is forced to have more babies than she wants.
She is old and near death. As this species ages, it's skin turns red.
She's working on a revival strategy and it seems to be working, whatever it is.
On the other hand, some things never change, and age may be taking it's toll on her regardless of her attempts at eternal youth.
This one is a spring chicken flush with the green-ness of youth. She's healthy, energetic and down with the plan to repopulate her corner of the forest.
She is still working on her theories for a fountain of youth. This is an improvement over her previous attempt.
She's still out having a good time and strutting her stuff. Youth is not wasted on the young.
She met a particularly fertile male.
The fountain of youth is improving! See how happy she is?!
She's getting tired after all that fertility. Her score is dropping.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?

feedback_text.text: I never noticed the different toe shapes, and the difference in the 2 shades of red and 2 shades of green were so faint as to not be evident when not seeing them side by side.
